violations.description: General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Observed employee handling personal items and returning to handling food without washing hands.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Boxes of food stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Inside of the reach-in coolers need to be cleaned.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitizer solution for three compartment sink was not maintained at the proper concentration. The sanitizer cycle on the dish machine was not working and measured at 0ppm.
Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. The faucet on the 3 comp sink was leaking at the time of inspection.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. The handwashing sink behind the front counter did not have any disposable towels available.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Walk in floors need to be cleaned.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --Employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. Employee clothing was hung up on the rack that held clean dishes and utensils.
Use of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not used and/or applied according to manufacturer's label. Sanitizer bucket was measuring at >500ppm.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. Wet towel found on the bakery table during inspection. Wet wiping cloths should be stored in a sanitizing solution between uses or laundered.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Portable fan blade cover was found dirty during inspection. This should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of individual disposable towels, continuous towel supply system, or heated air-drying device. Paper towel dispenser for the hand sink in the dish area was found empty. Paper towels should be available at all times.
